items.headline: Victor Wembanyama, Luka Doncic, Kawhi Leonard Lead NBA PER In March
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama, Luka Doncic, Kawhi Leonard Lead NBA PER In March — Victor Wembanyama finished the month of March with the highest PER in the NBA with a mark of 32.3 ahead of Luka Doncic and Kawhi Leonard at 30.3. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, who remains the betting favorite to win NBA MVP ahead of Wembanyama, ranked fourth at 29.7. Over 15 games in March , Wembanyama averaged 26.8 points, 12.0 rebo

items.headline: How NBA Players Adapt to the Digital Era Through Data and Lifestyle Changes
items.blurb: How NBA Players Adapt to the Digital Era Through Data and Lifestyle Changes — Source The NBA has changed from instinct-driven play to systems built on data. Teams now use tracking tools, which records player speed, distance, shot quality, and spacing on every possession. Coaches rely on this data to plan lineups and tactics. It is not only about numbers on a screen. Daily habits also shift. Sleep,

items.headline: TC Swirsky To Join Mercury As Assistant
items.blurb: TC Swirsky To Join Mercury As Assistant — Washington Wizards' assistant coach TC Swirsky is set to join Nate Tibbetts' staff with the Phoenix Mercury, reported Michael Scotto of HoopsHype. Swirsky was previously the head coach of the G League's Memphis Hustle.

items.headline: Anthony Davis Cleared For Light Contact
items.blurb: Anthony Davis Cleared For Light Contact — The Washington Wizards cleared Anthony Davis for light contact, the team announced on Tuesday. Davis has been out due to ligament damage in his left hand. Davis has appeared in 20 games this season. The Wizards are currently 17-58 and tied for the worst record in the league.

items.blurb: J.B. Bickerstaff After Pistons Clinch First Division Title In 18 Years: 'We Expect More' — The Detroit Pistons clinched their first division title since the 2007-08 season following a 127-116 win over the Toronto Raptors on Tuesday night. items.headline: UConn-Duke Averages 13.4M Viewers
items.blurb: UConn-Duke Averages 13.4M Viewers — Sunday's regional final between UConn and Duke averaged 13.4 million viewers on CBS. That number is up 15% from last year's Michigan State-Auburn matchup, and is the third largest audience for an individual game before the Final Four since 2021. items.headline: NBA Highlights: Trail Blazers 114, Clippers 104
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Trail Blazers 114, Clippers 104 — Deni Avdija had a 28-point, 11-rebound double-double and Jrue Holiday dropped a game-high 30 points to lead the Portland Trail blazers past the Los Angeles Clippers 114-104.

items.headline: Raptors can’t overcome shooting gap in all-too-familiar loss to Pistons
items.blurb: Raptors can’t overcome shooting gap in all-too-familiar loss to Pistons — There is one statistical category that is pretty hard to work around, and the Raptors struggle with it regularly. Such was the case on Tuesday’s loss, as Toronto was outshot comfortably by the Pistons.

items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Lakers 127, Cavaliers 113 — Luka Doncic scored 42 points and added 12 assists to lead the Los Angeles Lakers to a 127-113 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers.

items.headline: Did Luka Doncic commit a strategic technical foul?
items.blurb: Did Luka Doncic commit a strategic technical foul? — Luka Doncic spent weeks on the verge of a one-game suspension after receiving his 15th technical foul of the season. He finally drew that suspension for the Los Angeles Lakers' easiest remaining game, against the lowly Washington Wizards.

items.headline: James finishes ferociously at the rim off of Reaves fast-break dish
items.blurb: James finishes ferociously at the rim off of Reaves fast-break dish — The Los Angeles Lakers’ Deandre Ayton deflects a pass from James Harden which ignites a fast-break, leading to an Austin Reaves lob to a trailing LeBron James, who rises high and punches the ball through the net with one hand.

items.headline: James denies Mitchell with patented chase-down block seconds after tip
items.blurb: James denies Mitchell with patented chase-down block seconds after tip — The Los Angeles Lakers’ LeBron James tracks down the Cleveland Cavaliers’ Donovan Mitchell right off the jump ball and swats the star’s finger roll attempt out of bounds just five seconds into the game.

items.blurb: Jaden Ivey To Receive Full Salary Despite Being Waived By Bulls For Conduct Issues — Jaden Ivey will collect his entire $10.1 million salary this season despite the Chicago Bulls releasing the guard for conduct detrimental to the team, sources briefed on the matter confirmed. items.blurb: Stephen Curry Participates In Full Scrimmage, Eyes Sunday Return — Golden State Warriors guard Stephen Curry received medical clearance to participate in a five-on-five scrimmage Tuesday, marking the most significant milestone in his recovery from a persistent right knee injury. items.headline: NBA Highlights: Pistons 127, Raptors 116
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Pistons 127, Raptors 116 — Jalen Duren dominated with 31 points while knocking down 12 of his 13 field-goal attempts and Duncan Robinson drilled five three-pointers and finished with 19 points to lead the Detroit Pistons to a 127-116 win over the Toronto Raptors.

items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Rockets 111, Knicks 94 — Kevin Durant registered 27 points, eight assists, and six rebounds, and Alperen Sengun added a double-double of 13 points and 10 assists to lead the Houston Rockets to a 111-94 victory over the New York Knicks.

items.headline: Lucas Williamson, Girzzlies Sign 10-Day Contract
items.blurb: Lucas Williamson, Girzzlies Sign 10-Day Contract — Wing Lucas Williamson has signed a 10-day contract with the Memphis Grizzlies. Williamson (6-4, 205) has appeared in 46 games (15 starts) for the Memphis Hustle and Windy City Bulls during the 2025-26 NBA G League Tip-Off Tournament and regular season has averaged 7.2 points, 2.3 rebounds, 1.3 assists and 1.1 steals in 23.7 minutes. items.headline: NBA Highlights: Bucks 123, Mavericks 99
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Bucks 123, Mavericks 99 — Ryan Rollins registered 24 points, nine assists, and seven rebounds, Kyle Kuzma added 20 points, and AJ Green scored 17 points off the bench to lead the Milwaukee Bucks to a comfortable 123-99 victory over the Dallas Mavericks.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Hornets 117, Nets 86
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Hornets 117, Nets 86 — Brandon Miller scored 25 points and grabbed three rebounds as the Charlotte Hornets defeated the Brooklyn Nets 117-86.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Magic 115, Suns 111
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Magic 115, Suns 111 — Desmond Bane scored 21 points, Jalen Suggs registered 20 points, and Paolo Banchero tallied 19 points, nine rebounds, and eight assists to lead the Orlando Magic to a 115-111 victory over the Phoenix Suns.

items.blurb: Raptors assistant Batiste cherishes time with top baseball prospect son — On a late-March Saturday in Phoenix, Toronto Raptors assistant coach Mike Batiste enjoyed a gift from the NBA schedule to be cherished. A chance to watch his son, a top baseball prospect, play, writes Michael Grange.
